title: [FIRRTL] Regreset canonicalizer replaces register with value of different type
body:
If a regreset has a constantly-high reset, then we can replace the register with its reset value. However, if the reset value's type does
not match the type of the register, then we end up replacing the register with a value of a different type. When the register is driven by a matching connect, this is invalid IR.

Here is an example:
    
```mlir
firrtl.circuit "Scratch" {
  firrtl.module @Scratch(out %out : !firrtl.uint<2>) {

    // The reset value is 1-bit, but the register is 2-bits.
    %c0_ui1 = firrtl.constant 0 : !firrtl.uint<1>
    %c0_ui2 = firrtl.constant 0 : !firrtl.uint<2>
    %c0_clock = firrtl.specialconstant 0 : !firrtl.clock
    %c1_asyncreset = firrtl.specialconstant 1 : !firrtl.asyncreset
    %reg = firrtl.regreset %c0_clock, %c1_asyncreset, %c0_ui1 : !firrtl.clock, !firrtl.asyncreset, !firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.uint<2>

    // The canonicalizer ends up driving the 1-bit reset to the 2-bit output port using a matching connect.
    firrtl.matchingconnect %out, %reg : !firrtl.uint<2>
    firrtl.matchingconnect %reg, %c0_ui2 : !firrtl.uint<2>
  }
}
```

After canonicalization:
```mlir
// -----// IR Dump After Canonicalizer Failed (canonicalize) //----- //
"firrtl.module"() <{annotations = [], convention = #firrtl<convention internal>, layers = [], portAnnotations = [], portDirections = array<i1: true>, portLocations = [loc("./scratch2.mlir":2:30)], portNames = ["out"], portSymbols = [], portTypes = [!firrtl.uint<2>], sym_name = "Scratch"}> ({
^bb0(%arg0: !firrtl.uint<2>):
  %0 = "firrtl.constant"() <{value = 0 : ui1}> : () -> !firrtl.uint<1>
  "firrtl.matchingconnect"(%arg0, %0) : (!firrtl.uint<2>, !firrtl.uint<1>) -> ()
}) : () -> ()
```

Run using:
```
circt-opt --pass-pipeline="builtin.module(firrtl.circuit(firrtl.module(canonicalize{max-iterations=10 max-num-rewrites=-1 region-simplify=disabled test-convergence=false top-down=true})))" ./scratch2.mlir --mlir-print-ir-after-failure
```
